revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Brunnenkur]][[eo:Akvoterapio]][[ja:飲泉]][[pl:Krenoterapia]][[sv:Surbrunn]]
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:10, 20 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Expand German}} {{Expand Swedish}}
{{nihongo|'''Insen'''|飲泉|lit: drinking from spring}} is the process of drinking from a hot spring.<ref name=":1">[https://enjoyonsen.city.beppu-jp.com/onsen/drink-hot-spring-water/ You Can Drink Hot Spring Water? Japanese “Insen” - Enjoy Onsen]</ref> People often drink water from hot springs.<ref name=":1" /><ref>https://openwaterhq.com/swimming/is-hot-spring-water-safe-to-drink/</ref><ref>https://www.findinghotsprings.com/mag/8-suprising-health-benefits-natural-hot-springs</ref><ref name=":0">https://www.ncesc.com/can-you-drink-the-water-at-hot-springs/</ref><ref>https://roambliss.com/natural-hot-springs-truth-or-consequences/</ref><ref>https://hotspringhunt.com/blog/is-hot-spring-water-safe-to-drink/</ref><ref>https://www.waterworld.com/drinking-water-treatment/article/16190252/tasting-the-waters-of-hot-springs</ref><ref>https://hotspringslocator.com/hot-springs-risks</ref><ref>[https://www.hotspringjourneys.com/know-before-you-go/health-benefits Health Benefits of Hot Springs — Hot Spring Journeys]</ref>
It is considered generally safe to drink.<ref>[https://www.tapsafe.org/is-hot-springs-tap-water-safe-to-drink/ Is Hot Springs Tap Water Safe to Drink? - 2022]</ref> But doing so has numerous risks.<ref name=":0" /> The high mineral content of hot springs can disrupt the ionic balance in the body causing potentially serious injury.<ref name=":0" /> And as it is due to the ionic content rather than microbes, boiling the water does not make it more safe to drink.<ref name=":0" />
The Japanese Onsen associations bans people under 15 from drinking from hot springs unless prescribed by a doctor, as well as those suffering from {{ill|Dysphagia|en|Dysphagia|qid=Q623289|lt=dysphagia|1=Dysphagia}}.<ref>[https://www.spa.or.jp/en/caution_drinking/ Caution for drinking Onsen water]</ref>
The Japanese onsen association has a list of requirements and contraindications for Onsen drinking based around the ion content and people's conditions they suffer from.<ref>[https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/pdf/pamph_full_en.pdf Onsen_ABC]</ref>
Drinking from hot springs is not allowed in Tokyo<ref name=":2">https://doi.org/10.11390/onki.74.246</ref>
{{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} claims their weakly acidic water is effective for gout, and chronic allergies. <ref>https://www.ikaho-kankou.com/en/aboutikaho/nature/water/</ref> It is an {{ill|Chalybeate|en|Chalybeate|qid=Q3046709|lt=Iron-containing spring|1=Chalybeate springs}} ==Overview==
[[File:Yumura onsen21bs4592.jpg|thumb|220px|Drinking spring at [[:en:Yumura_Onsen_(Hyōgo)|Yumura Onsen]]]]
'''Insen''' (飲泉) refers to the practice of drinking {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} water, or seeking health benefits such as recovery from illness through this act.
Historically, in {{ill|balneotherapy|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/balneotherapy|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1004887}}, it was believed that drinking hot spring water enhanced the therapeutic effects beyond merely bathing. Carbonated springs, in particular, were revered as "sacred springs" and valued highly for their medicinal properties, sometimes even consumed as beverages. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} and {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q329174}}, carbonated springs were used as ingredients for {{ill|List of lemon-lime drink brands|en|List of lemon-lime drink brands|qid=Q1130245|lt=cider|1=List of lemon-lime drink brands}} beverages. A prominent example is "Arima Cider" from {{ill|Arima Onsen|en|Arima Onsen|qid=Q4790673|lt=Arima Onsen|1=Arima Onsen}}.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161005112130/https://arimaonsen.sakura.ne.jp/miyage_cider.htm 有馬サイダー] (Accessed October 4, 2016)</ref>
Today, it remains common to provide users with drinking cups ({{ill|Cup|en|Cup|qid=Q81727|lt=cups|1=Cup}}) or ladles ({{ill|Hishaku|en|Hishaku|qid=Q11533616|lt=ladles|1=Hishaku}}) near outlets of {{ill|open-air baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|ko|노천탕|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/open-air baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}, or to separately install faucets ({{ill|Tap (valve)|en|Tap (valve)|qid=Q656656|lt=faucets|1=Tap (valve)}}) specifically as drinking facilities. However, depending on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|lt=spring quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=}}, some waters are not suitable for drinking due to strong acidity, high salt concentrations, or toxic elements such as {{ill|Mercury (element)|en|Mercury (element)|qid=Q925|lt=mercury|1=Mercury (element)}} or {{ill|Arsenic|en|Arsenic|qid=Q871|lt=arsenic|1=Arsenic}}. Additionally, hygienic concerns arise from drinking water taken from outlets of {{ill|circulating baths|ja|循環風呂|1=Recirculating Baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circulating baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Recirculating Baths|qq=}} or from springs lacking health department approval for drinking.
In Tokyo, drinking hot spring water is not permitted, and there may be regional restrictions as well.<ref name="\"naid10029520830\"">{{Cite journal|language=ja|author1=前田眞治|author2=市川勝|author3=原麻理子|author4=櫻井好美|author5=平野絵美|author6=小暮英輔|author7=山本潤|date=2011-08-01|title=東京都23区内の温泉と期待される温泉医学的効果|journal=日本温泉気候物理医学会雑誌|volume=74|issue=4|pages=246-255|doi=10.11390/onki.74.246|url=https://doi.org/10.11390/onki.74.246}}</ref> The separate table of a {{ill|hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring analysis report|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉分析書}} lists contraindications for drinking spring water, which can be referred to when determining suitability.
Regarding the effects of drinking spring water, a 2017 survey identified 12 studies investigating potential health benefits in gastrointestinal, gallbladder-pancreatic, and sugar metabolism areas. Although these studies suggest possible benefits, further research is required due to the diverse compositions of spring waters.<ref>https://www.paz.ac.jp/library/lib/acaach/bulletin/22/PAZ22-02.pdf</ref>
== Claimed benefits == Advocates claim drinking hot spring water can treat the following conditions<ref name=":1" />
* constipation * atrophic gastritis * gastroduodenal ulcer * reflux esophagitis * low gastrointestinal function
* type 2 diabetes * hyperuricemia * biliary dysfunction * high cholesterol
* iron-deficiency anemia
== Examples of Drinking Springs ==
<gallery> File:Atsumi-spa-insennjo.JPG|alt=|{{ill|Atsumi Onsen|ja|温海温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsumi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to あつみ温泉}} File:Kin-no-yu Arima Onsen01s3200.jpg|{{ill|Arima Onsen|en|Arima Onsen|qid=Q4790673|lt=Arima Onsen|1=Arima Onsen}}「{{ill|Golden Spring|ja|金の湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Golden Spring|qq=}}」 File:Kinosakionsen st03st3200.jpg|{{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|zh|城崎溫泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}|alt=城崎温泉 File:Shima Onsen Gunma 01.jpg|alt=四万温泉|{{ill|Shima Onsen|en|Shima Onsen|qid=Q30595841|lt=Shima Onsen|1=Shima Onsen}} File:Hamasaka onsen02s2048.jpg|alt=浜坂温泉|{{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|qq=}} File:Hot Springs Water Fontain.jpg|alt=ホットスプリングス|{{ill|Hot Springs, Arkansas|en|Hot Springs, Arkansas|qid=Q79924|lt=Hot Springs, Arkansas|1=Hot Springs, Arkansas}} </gallery>
== See Also == * {{ill|Mineral water|en|Mineral water|qid=Q178921|lt=Mineral water|1=Mineral water}} – In Europe and elsewhere, spring waters have also historically been consumed for health and healing properties.
* {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}}, {{ill|Spa town|en|Spa town|qid=Q4946461|lt=Onsen town|1=Spa town}}, {{ill|Public bath|ja|外湯|1=Sotoyu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoyu|qq=}}, {{ill|Open-air bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|ko|노천탕|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-air bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}, {{ill|Ashiyu|en|Ashiyu|qid=Q725522|lt=Ashiyu|1=Ashiyu}}, {{ill|Onsen Rankings|ja|温泉番付|1=onsen banzuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Rankings|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Rankings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11562960}} * {{ill|List of hot springs in Japan|en|List of hot springs in Japan|qid=Q11086467|lt=List of hot springs in Japan|1=List of hot springs in Japan}}
== References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal |language=ja |author1=前田眞治 |author2=市川勝 |author3=原麻理子 |author4=櫻井好美 |author5=平野絵美 |author6=小暮英輔 |author7=山暮英輔 |date= |title=飲泉に関する禁忌について |url=https://doi.org/10.11390/onki.74.246 |journal=日本温泉気候物理医学会雑誌 |volume=74 |pages=246–253 |doi=10.11390/onki.74.246}} * [http://www.spa.or.jp/onsen/528/ Drinking Spring Water - Japan Onsen Association] (accessed October 4, 2016)
== External Links == * [http://www.ikaho-kankou.com/nature_water.cfm Ikaho Water - Shibukawa Ikaho Onsen Tourism Association] (Retrieved October 4, 2016)
{{Onsen}}
